skill.name: market-research-reports
skill.description: Generate comprehensive market research reports with structured analysis, LaTeX formatting, market sizing frameworks, competitive analysis, and visual planning guidance.
skill.summary: Create structured market research reports with analysis frameworks, visuals, and LaTeX output.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high matches, but sampled context shows most are false positives from TAM/SAM/SOM terminology, Markdown code fences, LaTeX commands, and relative figure paths. The confirmed risk is legitimate local command execution in a visual generation helper, plus filesystem writes for generated figures. No prompt injection text, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Visual Generation Executes Commands
medium_findings.description: The helper builds an argv list for local scientific-schematics or generate-image scripts and runs it with subprocess.run. This is a legitimate feature, but it executes local code and passes user-provided topics into prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct and confirmed. Risk is moderated because arguments are passed as a list and target scripts are fixed local paths.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Directory Writes
medium_findings.description: The visual helper accepts an output directory and creates it before writing generated image files. This is expected behavior for report generation, but users should run it only in trusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The output directory is clearly user-controlled and mkdir is called. No evidence shows destructive writes or hidden file access.

low_findings.title: Markdown and LaTeX Syntax Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are inline code, fenced examples, or LaTeX snippets in documentation. They document intended report and visual workflows rather than hidden command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples and LaTeX package usage. They are not dynamic execution sites by themselves.

low_findings.title: Market Research Terms Misclassified as Sensitive System Artifacts
low_findings.description: Critical Windows SAM findings are false positives for Serviceable Addressable Market in TAM/SAM/SOM analysis. No evidence of Windows credential database access was found in the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The acronym SAM is explicitly defined as Serviceable Addressable Market. The context is market sizing, not operating system credential files.

low_findings.title: Relative Figure Paths and Acronyms Misclassified
low_findings.description: Path traversal and weak crypto findings are caused by normal relative figure paths and market or formatting abbreviations. The sampled lines do not read arbitrary files or implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths point to report figure assets, and the style package line is package metadata. No file traversal sink or crypto operation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Visual Generation Executes Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper builds an argv list for local scientific-schematics or generate-image scripts and runs it with subprocess.run. This is a legitimate feature, but it executes local code and passes user-provided topics into prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct and confirmed. Risk is moderated because arguments are passed as a list and target scripts are fixed local paths.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear market analysis that combines sizing, competitors, risks, and recommendations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce structured reports with visuals and LaTeX formatting.
actual_capabilities: Builds long-form market research report outlines and section guidance.
Uses TAM, SAM, SOM, SWOT, PESTLE, BCG Matrix, and Porters Five Forces frameworks.
Provides LaTeX report templates and professional formatting guidance.
Defines prompts and script support for market visuals and diagrams.
Structures executive summaries, market sizing, competitive analysis, risks, and recommendations.
Supports source attribution guidance for public market data.
limitations: It does not verify market data unless connected to research tools or provided sources.
Generated numbers should be checked against current primary or trusted secondary sources.
PDF output depends on local LaTeX and image-generation tooling.
The visual helper can run local scripts and write image files.

use_cases.title: Prepare Market Entry Analysis
use_cases.description: Create a structured report for evaluating a new geography, segment, or product category.

use_cases.title: Support Investment Diligence
use_cases.description: Organize market size, growth drivers, competitor positions, risks, and investment implications.

use_cases.title: Build Executive Research Briefs
use_cases.description: Turn research notes into an executive-ready market report with visuals and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Market Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a market research report outline for [market]. Include scope, audience, key questions, required visuals, and data needs.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Market Size
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop the TAM, SAM, and SOM section for [market]. Explain assumptions, data sources, calculations, and confidence limits.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Competitors
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a competitive landscape section for [market]. Include market share, positioning, Porters Five Forces, and strategic implications.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Final Report Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a full market research report for [market] using the provided sources. Include visuals, LaTeX formatting, risks, recommendations, and citations.

output_examples.input: A founder asks for a market entry report on battery recycling in North America.
output: Executive summary with market snapshot and investment thesis.
TAM, SAM, and SOM analysis with assumptions and source notes.
Competitive landscape, risk heatmap, and prioritized entry recommendations.

output_examples.input: An analyst needs a report section on cloud security market competition.
output: Porters Five Forces assessment with buyer, supplier, entrant, substitute, and rivalry ratings.
Competitive positioning matrix with leading vendors and challengers.
Strategic implications for product differentiation and channel strategy.

output_examples.input: A product team needs visuals for an executive market report.
output: Visual plan covering growth trajectory, TAM/SAM/SOM, risk heatmap, and positioning matrix.
Image prompts for scientific-schematics and generate-image tools.
LaTeX figure placement guidance with captions and source attribution.

best_practices: Provide current sources, market scope, geography, and audience before drafting.
Validate all market size estimates and growth rates before sharing the report.
Run local visual scripts only in a trusted workspace with expected output paths.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated market numbers as verified facts without source review.
Do not run visual generation commands from untrusted modified skill files.
Do not publish reports that lack assumptions, citations, and uncertainty notes.

faq.question: Does this skill gather live market data?
faq.answer: It provides research structure and source guidance. Live data gathering depends on available research tools and supplied sources.

faq.question: Can it create PDF reports?
faq.answer: It includes LaTeX templates and formatting guidance. PDF generation requires a local LaTeX toolchain.

faq.question: What analysis frameworks are included?
faq.answer: It supports TAM, SAM, SOM, SWOT, PESTLE, BCG Matrix, Porters Five Forces, and risk analysis.

faq.question: Can it generate visuals?
faq.answer: It includes visual prompts and a helper script that calls local image-generation skills when installed.

faq.question: Is it suitable for investment diligence?
faq.answer: Yes, as a report framework. Analysts must verify data, assumptions, and conclusions independently.

faq.question: What security review is needed before use?
faq.answer: Review local script execution and output paths before running visual generation commands.
